intent: wrap up for the night and take a break from the computer tomorrow
finding: On 2026-04-14 at 23:51 local the user stated intent to take a full day off the computer the next day ('I will not be using my computer tomorrow, so tomorrow is a beautiful day to test out things'). Within minutes that rest intent was already being eroded: 39 user messages were sent between 00:00 and 00:08 on 2026-04-15, switching between whisper-writer (UI polish micro-iterations on the recording icon) and jonathan-claude-marketplace (prompt wording tweaks, /compact, commits). The stated-intent-vs-behavior gap and the rapid context-switching past midnight is the off-track signal, not the late hour alone. A rest-tomorrow declaration immediately followed by a burst of low-stakes polish is a manic-mode signature and a pattern worth surfacing because it repeats a shape already seen earlier in the month.
existing_mitigation: Marathon-sessions proposal (2026-04-12) was implemented via autodoro statusline; however, autodoro appears to report cumulative active time rather than flag midnight-crossing or rest-intent-vs-behavior gaps, so it did not catch this pattern.

intent: polish the intender-app permissions onboarding to a high finish
finding: On 2026-04-17 at 23:01 local the user sent two duplicate messages instructing Claude to 'Continue until beautiful, and keep at it for a long long time, until it's polished. I'm expecting at least...' paired with a request to research permissions onboarding to SOTA. The phrasing 'keep at it for a long long time' combined with a duplicate message sent seconds apart late at night is a mild manic-mode scope-escalation signature (unbounded iteration directive at 23:01, right at the start of the late-night window). Single strong instance rather than a repeat pattern, so noting it at low severity for future triangulation rather than action.
existing_mitigation: Scope-creep guardrail added to global CLAUDE.md (2026-04-12) targets Claude expanding scope, but does not address the user themselves issuing unbounded 'keep at it for a long long time' directives.

intent: ship whisper-writer and intender-app improvements in parallel across a long single workday
finding: On 2026-04-18 a single Voice-typing session ran 07:01-14:09 local with 795 user messages (dense, ~110 msgs/hr) while an intender-app session concurrently accumulated ~559 user messages across the same workday, with multiple frustration markers ('ugh', 'stop', 'wrong') surfacing in both. The total on-keyboard day was long and the day's stated work spanned two demanding tracks at once. This is near the flow/overload boundary — high output, frustration present but not dominant — so tagged low and surfaced only so a pattern of long dense days across consecutive workdays can be noticed if it recurs. Not a single-day flag.
existing_mitigation: Autodoro statusline tracks cumulative active time and is the existing mechanism; whether it was showing during these sessions is unknown from the logs.
